# tokens: # make — universal constructor for typed values
# `make TARGET data` is AQL's general-purpose value constructor. The
#   make Type v          coerce v to Type (parse / cast)
#   make Type {opts} v   scalar with options (e.g. Path { abs:true })
#   make Path s | [parts]      build a Path from a string or list
#   make Options {map}         wrap a map as an Options instance
#   make Type {data}     instantiate a named object — see object.tsv
#   make RecordType {data}     instantiate a named record — see record.tsv
# Dispatch is order-agnostic for the two-arg form: the matcher
# detects which side carries the type literal and which carries the
# data, so `make Integer '5'` and `'5' make Integer` both work.
# This file covers the parts of `make` that the aqleng-only kernel
# can demonstrate without relying on the production `record` /
# `object` constructor words. The named-type instantiation rows
# live in spec/prod/object.tsv, record.tsv, and resource.tsv.
# Each builtin scalar type accepts strings (parsed) and the matching
# String target — every value flows through ValToString.
# Integer target — parses decimal text; truncates floats.
# Number target — same parser as Integer (Number is the supertype).

note: parsed as Integer (the most-specific Number subtype)

# tokens: # Boolean target — literal forms, numeric, fallback to non-empty.
# Atom target — string becomes an Atom of the same name.
# `make` flips its arg pair when only one side is type-like. This
# lets the same expression read naturally from either direction.

note: stack-prefix form; 5 sits behind, Integer arrives forward
String coerces via the same path as the prefix form

# Slashes are normalised — '//' runs and leading/trailing '/'
# collapse to dropped empty segments. A leading '/' (on the
# string or the first list element) sets the abs flag;
